data.title: Neumora Therapeutics Warns Nasdaq Delisting Risk Could Hit Liquidity, Share Price, and Capital Access
data.description:
Neumora Therapeutics, Inc. (NMRA) has disclosed a new risk, in the Share Price & Shareholder Rights category.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Neumora Therapeutics, Inc. faces a material risk that failure to maintain its Nasdaq Global Select Market listing, including the $1.00 minimum bid price, could lead to delisting and significantly reduced liquidity. Such an outcome would likely depress trading price and volume, narrow the investor base, constrain financing options, and make it harder for stockholders to sell their shares.

Although the company recently regained compliance with Nasdaq’s bid price requirement after a prior deficiency notice, there is no assurance it will sustain compliance with this or other continued listing standards. Persistent or future listing challenges could weaken market visibility, reduce broker-dealer and market maker participation, and impair overall capital markets access for the company.

The average NMRA stock price target is $10.80, implying 459.59% upside potential.

To learn more about Neumora Therapeutics, Inc.’s risk factors, click here.

Chemed President and CEO Kevin J. McNamara has recently sold 1,500 shares of Chemed stock in a transaction valued at $554,055, according to the latest filing. This insider sale highlights a notable move by the company’s top executive in the open market.

Recent Updates on CHE stock

Chemed shares were influenced by its latest earnings call, which mixed solid VITAS admissions growth and reduced Florida Medicare cap risk with weaker profitability at both VITAS and Roto‑Rooter. Analysts’ price target updates likely reflected this contrast between improving hospice fundamentals and near‑term margin pressure from short‑stay mix, tariff sensitivity, and ongoing sector headwinds.

The Q4 earnings miss, sharp EBITDA compression at Roto‑Rooter driven by higher marketing and insurer‑driven write‑offs, and transition costs expected to weigh on H1 2026 also factored into revised outlooks. At the same time, management’s 2026 guidance, new Florida market entry, balance‑sheet strength, and detailed remediation plans for billing, mix, and SEO supported a more constructive medium‑term view, tempering negative reactions to recent performance.

Spark’s Take on CHE Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CHE is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by strong multi-year financial quality (profitability, low leverage, and reliable cash generation). Technicals are supportive with a positive trend and reasonable momentum. Offsetting factors are a moderate valuation and a mixed earnings-call outlook: improving 2026 guidance but recent operational/margin issues and an execution-dependent, back-half-weighted recovery.
